mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic deceleration, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with 20-day forward returns showing no significant positive skew when both series trend upward simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production signals manufacturing contraction and weak aggregate demand, which reduces equity valuations and generates negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The mechanism is rejected if 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where INDPRO declines for three consecutive months, or if the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes outside recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, stimulating risk appetite and equity buying, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward testing shows negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the real rate differential fails to predict equity direction with a Rank IC above 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market and rising consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or if the series shows no statistically significant predictive power over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 2.0 percent annually, economic momentum stalls and corporate earnings expectations are downgraded, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below the 2.0 percent threshold, or the threshold signal produces a negative Rank IC in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and unemployment rate declines indicates supply-side bottlenecks and overheating capacity, which raises input costs and compresses margins, generating negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction signal is rejected if 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of periods exhibiting this divergence, or if the joint predictor fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.02 in walk-forward testing.
